subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: dy/dx = e^y(3x²-6x) — Tangent Line Approx & Separation of Variables
stimulus: Consider the differential equation $\frac{dy}{dx} = e^y(3x^2 - 6x)$. Let $y = f(x)$ be the particular solution to the differential equation that passes through $(1, 0)$.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $f$ at the point $(1, 0)$. Use the tangent line to approximate $f(1.2)$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Slope at $(1,0)$: $e^0(3-6) = -3$. Tangent line: $y = -3(x-1)$. $f(1.2) \approx -0.6$.

parts.prompt: Find $y = f(x)$, the particular solution to the differential equation that passes through $(1, 0)$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Includes constant of integration and uses initial condition $(1, 0)$: $-e^0 = 1 - 3 + C \Rightarrow -1 = -2 + C \Rightarrow C = 1$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int e^{-y}\, dy = \int(3x^2-6x)\, dx \Rightarrow -e^{-y} = x^3-3x^2+C$. Using $(1,0)$: $C=1$. Solving: $y = -\ln(-x^3+3x^2-1)$. Valid on interval containing $x=1$ where $-x^3+3x^2-1>0$. 0/6 if no separation; max 3/6 if no constant.
